purpose: Machine-readable dependency policy for the ten OPL Framework source modules under src/modules.
machine_boundary: This contract governs physical source-module imports only. It does not define runtime truth, domain truth, owner receipts, typed blockers, release readiness, product packaging, or a new Framework module.

deep_cross_module_imports.reason: Cross-module imports must use the target module public index or a thin public entry under public/**. The previous transition baseline has been migrated, so new internal cross-module imports fail the default source module boundary check.

module_dependency_cycles.reason: The current Framework source has public-entry-level module cycles. The checker reports strongly connected module components by default and fails only when this field or --strict-cycles asks for strict enforcement.

done_evidence_fields: module_entrypoints.expected_count=10 with no missing, mismatched, or unexpected module roots
root_ts.top_level_count=0 and target CLI entrypoint exists
partial_gaps: module public index files are still broad public re-export surfaces; strict imports prove import route legality, not minimal API shape
next_forbidden_dependency_candidates are advisory until each pair has a migration path and zero accepted callers

forbidden_dependencies.reason: Charter is the framework naming and contract owner; it must not depend on operator projection surfaces.
Charter must not depend on agent-improvement surfaces.
Charter must not depend on distribution or connector surfaces.
Ledger is the refs-only evidence owner; it must not depend on execution, queue, worker, or recovery surfaces.
Stagecraft designs stage policy and work orders; Runway consumes them for execution rather than becoming a Stagecraft prerequisite.
Workspace owns protocol and file lifecycle projection; Console consumes workspace projections for operator surfaces.
Foundry Kernel owns producer-neutral FoundryRun orchestration; Console-owned readiness and operator projections must not become a Foundry prerequisite.

next_forbidden_dependency_candidates.reason: Runway owns durable execution and runtime state; Console should consume Runway projections rather than becoming a runtime prerequisite.
next_forbidden_dependency_candidates.promotion_gate: Split runtime tray/operator projection inputs before enforcing this direction.

next_forbidden_dependency_candidates.reason: Stagecraft owns stage policy and handoff design; Console should project stage readiness rather than feed Stagecraft behavior.
next_forbidden_dependency_candidates.promotion_gate: Move any operator narration or drilldown shape behind Stagecraft-owned public output or Console-owned consumer code.

next_forbidden_dependency_candidates.reason: Connect owns directly callable connector and distribution surfaces; it must not require Console implementation to expose connector capability.
next_forbidden_dependency_candidates.promotion_gate: Keep connector descriptors and install/sync surfaces callable through Connect public entries without Console imports.

next_forbidden_dependency_candidates.reason: Ledger owns refs-only evidence and lineage; connector lookup or distribution behavior should not be a Ledger prerequisite.
next_forbidden_dependency_candidates.promotion_gate: Confirm evidence records store refs only and route connector/provider work through Connect callers.

next_forbidden_dependency_candidates.reason: Pack owns declarative ABI and generated surfaces; operator presentation should consume Pack outputs rather than shape Pack internals.
next_forbidden_dependency_candidates.promotion_gate: Extract any operator-only rendering or narration into Console consumers before enforcing this pair.
